name: Chuseok
traditions: Ancestral memorial rites (charye); making songpyeon (rice cakes); traditional folk games; family gatherings; visiting hometowns.
historicalBackground: Chuseok dates back to the Three Kingdoms period (57 BCE–668 CE). It was originally a harvest thanksgiving, with roots in the ancient moon worship festival of Hangawi.
description: Korea's harvest moon thanksgiving, when families reunite to honor ancestors, make songpyeon rice cakes, and celebrate the autumn harvest.
